channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Car abandoned in The Gardens - flat tyre and no MOT, 31st March
item.description:
This car has not moved for 3 months. It has no MOT and a flat tyre. LY10HAO

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Gardens, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At the corner of Norman park by the bins and on the land leased by council to the Bromley community running associations d community., 31st March
item.description:
Every time I visit the part there is litter on both sides of fence between the community Centre and the park. It is by a litter bin which the birds take out food from. The bin needs a lid to reduce the amount of litter taken out and the litter needs cleaning up every month. It’s been going on for the past year and it’s quite honestly an eyesore. Would’ve great if something could’ve done about a litter pick on a regular basis. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayes Lane, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of builders rubbish, 31st March
item.description:
The insulation and other rubbish from the photos in a previous report is still here, despite the report being marked as fixed. There are a few different rubbish dumps that have been made along here if you turn left past the entrance to the cemetery and go down the track towards the woods. Please clear all of it up!
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodbrook Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden of 11 and 15 Oseney crescent, 31st March
item.description:
Resident has reported this for six years, overgrown trees in neighbours garden which is overhanging into his garden.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Caversham Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins and fly tipping at 11-22 Craven Close, Castlewood Road London N16 6BP, 31st March
item.description:
Flytipping and overflowing bins for the last 2 days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Craven Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: East Dulwich Grove, 31st March
item.description:
Road breaking up between centre island and 1 Delft Way on East Dulwich Grove. Making houses vibrate

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Arnhem Way, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In carriageway opposite tfl depot, 31st March
item.description:
There is a pothole approximately 30cms diameter 10-15cms deep in the carriageway at the midpoint of junction with traffic heading north from Carlton vale, and Kilburn lane, there are two or three, but this particular one is a dangerous hazard, and requires urgent attention
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Premier Corner, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Both trees are located on the right grass space from the walk to St. Mary's Cray Station m the walk, 31st March
item.description:
Located in front of 56 Sayes Court Road BR5 two young trees on a public grass rise needs to be replanted due to one been totally damaged and another one appears to be dead

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 63, Sayes Court Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 50cm pothole on crossroads between Exford Road and Senlac Road, 31st March
item.description:
Large pothole being driven through at speed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Exford Road, Chinbrook, Grove Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Adjacent to the speed bump, deep thumping pothole, 31st March
item.description:
There is a pothole adjacent to a speed bump, which cannot be traversed safely at any speed. It causes crashing judder when riding over it at 2-3mph, and is almost hidden because of its location. It has been there for months, without repair
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Coverdale Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffitti, 31st March
item.description:
Blue on side wall of Card Factory.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 121, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car owner has dumped box of rubbish, 31st March
item.description:
Car owner has dumped a box of rubbish and drove off.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Straightsmouth, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Pothole on days lane road, 31st March
item.description:
Large pothole on days land road just after the turning to Oaklands avenue as you're driving from Blackfen to halfway street. serious danger to peoples cars and risk for motorbike riders falling off and seriously hurting themselves. unsure how long has been there but judging by the size of it, its been there a while

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 157, Days Lane, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Panels Dumped on Pavement at Wheathill Road, 31st March
item.description:
Two large panels have been dumped along the pavement at Wheathill Road, partially obstructing the footway. This is yet another instance of repeated flytipping at the same location. The ongoing lack of deterrence is clearly enabling this behaviour.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wheathill Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continual rubbish littering, 31st March
item.description:
Ongoing research nosh chucked into street bins over flowing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queen Elizabeths Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wheelie bins regularly left on corner of this road, 31st March
item.description:
Usually forgotten by refuse collectors - encouraging flytipping and use of bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Kempis Way, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Forst bike in a motor cycle vay, 31st March
item.description:
A forest bike has been parked in a motorcycles only bay for at least four days now. This has been abandoned in a location that is not meant to be used by these abandoned anywhere horrific bikes. Get rid of the Licensing because people do not know how to behave properly get rid of these bikes across the whole of the Lambeth borough. They cost more to the community then they give to the Council.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Aquinas Street, Waterloo, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Car, 31st March
item.description:
Car has been parked outside 58 Bexley lane for about 3 months now and not moved. Tyres are now flat. Vehicle is taxed and mot'd but looks like it has been abandoned

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bexley Lane, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Brick been removed crom floor, 31st March
item.description:
Brick been removed from floor

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lownds Court, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste, 31st March
item.description:
Litter and waste from collections. Please clear.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petersham Drive, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
